steps.title: Identify the grammatical function
steps.content: The blank must be a possessive word modifying narrative. The antecedent noun is poem, which is singular.

steps.title: Apply the singular possessive form
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: pronoun-antecedent agreement (possessive). Rule: a possessive pronoun must agree in number with its antecedent and must be a true possessive, not a contraction.
  • Choice A — "their": plural possessive; antecedent "poem" is singular. ✗
  • Choice B — "they're": contraction of "they are"; not a possessive at all. ✗
  • Choice C — "it's": contraction of "it is"; not a possessive. ✗
  • Choice D — "its": singular possessive pronoun; correctly refers to the singular noun "poem." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
